name: flow-nexus-swarm
description: Cloud-based AI swarm deployment and event-driven workflow automation with Flow Nexus platform
capabilities: Initialize AI agent swarms with hierarchical, mesh, ring, or star topologies
Spawn specialized agents for research, coding, analysis, optimization, and coordination
Create and execute event-driven workflows with parallel execution and dependencies
Use pre-built templates for full-stack dev, research, CI/CD, and data pipelines
Monitor swarm status, workflow metrics, and execution audit trails
Scale agent counts dynamically and coordinate multiple swarms for complex projects
author: DNYoussef
securitySummary: This skill is pure documentation describing external MCP tool calls. All 102 static findings are false positives: hash identifiers were misidentified as weak crypto, Markdown backticks as shell execution, and standard workflow IDs as reconnaissance. No executable code, scripts, network calls, or file system access exists. Previous audit confirmed safe status.
valueStatement: Managing multiple AI agents across complex workflows requires coordination, scalability, and reliable message passing between components. Flow Nexus Swarm provides cloud-based orchestration with event-driven workflows, pre-built templates, and intelligent agent assignment to scale agent deployments.
limitations: Requires separate Flow Nexus MCP server installation and active account
All execution happens on Flow Nexus cloud infrastructure, not locally
Agent limits and features depend on the Flow Nexus service tier
No local code execution or file system access within the skill itself

useCases.title: Full-Stack Delivery
useCases.description: Coordinate backend, frontend, database, and QA agents through parallel workflows with dependency management.

useCases.title: CI/CD Automation
useCases.description: Automate testing, building, security scanning, and multi-environment deployment with rollback support.

useCases.title: Distributed Research
useCases.description: Deploy mesh topology swarms for parallel information gathering and collaborative data analysis.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Swarm Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Initialize a hierarchical swarm with 6 agents using the balanced strategy, then spawn a coordinator, two coders, an analyst, and an optimizer.

promptTemplates.title: Workflow Creation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a workflow called Production Deploy with steps for linting, unit tests, build, security scan, and deployment. Configure exponential backoff retry with max 3 retries.

promptTemplates.title: Task Orchestration
promptTemplates.prompt: Orchestrate this task across 4 agents in parallel: Research the latest developments in quantum computing and analyze their implications for software security.

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Swarm Coordination
promptTemplates.prompt: Initialize three coordinated swarms: a mesh research swarm with 3 agents, a hierarchical development swarm with 6 agents, and a star testing swarm with 4 agents. Create a workflow that links them sequentially.

bestPractices: Start with simple topologies and increase complexity as you verify the workflow structure works correctly.
Define clear dependencies between workflow steps to ensure proper execution order and parallelize independent tasks.
Use the vector similarity matching option when assigning agents to optimize task-agent fit based on capability analysis.
antiPatterns: Avoid spawning more agents than needed without monitoring - excessive agents increase coordination overhead and cost.
Do not create complex workflows without proper error handling, retry policies, and timeout configurations.
Avoid skipping resource cleanup - always destroy swarms and workflows when they are no longer needed.

faq.question: Which AI tools are compatible with this skill?
faq.answer: The skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through the Flow Nexus MCP server interface.

faq.question: What are the agent and workflow limits?
faq.answer: Limits depend on your Flow Nexus service tier. Check flow-nexus.ruv.io for pricing and quotas.

faq.question: Can I use this with existing Claude Flow setups?
faq.answer: Yes. Flow Nexus integrates with Claude Flow hooks for pre-task and post-task coordination.

faq.question: Is my data safe with cloud-based execution?
faq.answer: Flow Nexus processes data on their cloud infrastructure. Review their privacy policy for compliance requirements.

faq.question: Why are my workflows failing?
faq.answer: Check agent availability, verify dependency configurations, and ensure retry policies are set for transient failures.

faq.question: How does this compare to local agent coordination?
faq.answer: Flow Nexus provides scalable cloud infrastructure for large swarms. Use local execution for smaller tasks with claude-flow.
